channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Solar-Eclipsing Probe Back From the Dead After a Month of Silence
item.description: An anomaly caused ESA's Proba-3 to ghost ground control, but now the spacecraft has finally made contact.

item.title: Sounds Like the ‘KPop Demon Hunters’ Directors Scored a Huge Payday for the Sequel
item.description: Netflix is digging deep to reward Maggie Kang and Chris Appelhans, the Oscar-winning directors of its biggest-ever movie.

item.title: Three ways AI is learning to understand the physical world
item.description: Large language models are running into limits in domains that require an understanding of the physical world — from robotics to autonomous driving to manufacturing. That constraint is pushing investors toward world models, with AMI Labs raising a $1.03 billion seed round shortly after World Labs secured $1 billion.Large language models (LLMs) excel at processing abstract knowledge through next-token prediction, but they fundamentally lack grounding in physical causality. They cannot reliably predict the physical consequences of real-world actions. AI researchers and thought leaders are increas...

item.title: Microsoft will yank Copilot from some Windows apps and let you move the taskbar again
item.description: After one too many of you threatened to switch to Linux, Microsoft has published a long list of changes it plans to make to Windows 11. In a lengthy blog titled "Our commitment to Windows quality," Pavan Davuluri, the executive vice president of Windows and Devices, said the company has spent a "great deal" of time in recent months reading feedback from users. "What came through was the voice of people who care deeply about Windows and want it to be better," he said. To that end, Windows Insiders can expect to see some of the changes Microsoft plans in response to all criticism begin rolling o...

item.title: Strava’s Fitness App Exposed the Real-Time Location of a French Aircraft Carrier
item.description: A sailor’s publicly shared workout made it possible to pinpoint the ship’s movements in the Mediterranean sea.

item.title: The White House proposes new AI policy framework that supersedes state laws
item.description: The White House has announced a new AI policy framework that calls for Congress to craft federal regulation that overrules state AI laws. The Trump administration has made multiple attempts to overrule more restrictive state-level AI regulation, but has failed so far, most notably in the passing of the “One Big Beautiful Bill.”The framework focuses on a variety of topics, covering everything from child privacy to the use of AI in the workforce. “Importantly, this framework can succeed only if it is applied uniformly across the United States,” The White House writes. “A patchwork of conflicting...

item.title: Nvidia CEO Wants Tech Execs to Stop Laying Off Workers and Scaring People
item.description: At GTC, Jensen Huang was on damage control for AI.

item.title: Physicists Say They’ve Cracked the Mystery of Volcanic Lightning
item.description: No divine sign, just science.

item.title: What happened at Nvidia GTC: NemoClaw, Robot Olaf, and a $1 trillion bet
item.description: CEO Jensen Huang took the stage at Nvidia’s GTC conference this week in his signature leather jacket to deliver a two-and-a-half-hour keynote, projecting $1 trillion in AI chip sales through 2027, declaring that every company needs an “OpenClaw strategy,” and closing with a rambling Olaf robot that had to get its mic cut. The message was hard to miss: Nvidia […]...

item.title: A new Nintendo Switch 2 could be the poster child for replaceable batteries
item.description: Nintendo plans to release a Switch 2 revision in the European Union that will let users replace their own batteries, Nikkei reports. The current version of the Switch 2 has a glued-in battery. But Nintendo apparently plans to make the change in order to comply with EU rules going into effect in February 2027 that […]

item.title: Microsoft a eu une illumination : Windows 11 va enfin intégrer ce que les utilisateurs réclament depuis des années
item.description: Windows 11 tel qu'on le connait aujourd'hui pourrait bien être très différent dans quelques mois. Microsoft a en effet annoncé une batterie impressionnante de changements et d'améliorations réclamés depuis des années par les utilisateurs.

item.title: Nevada Bans Kalshi for at Least the Next Two Weeks
item.description: The ban comes at a time when regulators and lawmakers are on high alert over prediction markets.

item.title: Future Sony PlayStation games will use AI to imagine new frames
item.description: Mark Cerny, the lead architect of the PlayStation 5 and PS5 Pro, told Digital Foundry that ML-based frame generation tech is coming to "PlayStation platforms" in the future, letting the game console use AI to imagine new frames between the ones it's actually rendering, which can create smoother perceived image quality while (typically) introducing some […]...

item.title: New Study Highlights Carbon Fiber Weakness That Sounds All Too Familiar After Titan Fiasco
item.description: Researchers planned to test how carbon fiber reacted to intense heat and humidity, but they found out something else entirely.

item.title: Could Agentic AI be the killer app for the 40-year old PC? AMD thinks so — and wants you to jump on the Agent Computer bandwagon before it is too late
item.description: AMD introduces Agentic AI on PCs, enabling autonomous task execution, persistent local models, and enhanced productivity for professionals and organizations.

item.title: Everything We Know About the Future of ‘Star Trek’
item.description: As the franchise prepares to celebrate 60 years of boldly going, its future in TV and film remains more in flux than it's been in a decade.

item.title: ‘We are only able to supply, for our key customers in the midterm, about 50% to two-thirds of their requirements’: Micron CEO forecasts production spend increase to meet the insane demand for memory – but the RAM crisis will only get worse
item.description: The RAM crisis is having a knock-on effect on the memory industry in general - but there could be an unlikely hero.

item.title: Why some brands are choosing slower, more expensive growth on purpose
item.description: Founders say sticking to their mission—even when it costs more—is what keeps customers coming back. When Rare Beauty, Bogg, and Goodles arrived in stores, they had to vie for shelf space with well-established brands making beauty products or beach bags or boxes of macaroni and cheese. But these brands quickly amassed cult-like followings by being very intentional with their missions to foster a sense of loyalty with customers....

item.title: Amidst legal turmoil, Kalshi is temporarily banned in Nevada
item.description: It's the latest escalation in a regulatory battle over the future of prediction markets.

item.title: The ‘bestie-fication’ of media is doing a disservice to everyone
item.description: Jake Shane’s awkwardness on the Oscars red carpet showed the shortcomings of using influencers as stand-ins for real journalists. If you tuned into the red carpet for the Academy Awards, you may have seen actress Julia Fox being interviewed by influencers Quen Blackwell and Jake Shane, who were at the awards show reporting for Vanity Fair.

item.title: Gamers Hate Nvidia's DLSS 5. Developers Aren’t Crazy About It, Either
item.description: Nvidia’s new AI upscaling gaming technology struck gamers as uncanny and off-putting. Developers don't seem to like it, either, but it could be “the default” in a few years.

item.title: Pinterest CEO calls on governments to ban social media for users under 16
item.description: Pinterest’s chief compares social media to tobacco and alcohol, saying children need similar protections.

item.title: Trump Proposes a ‘Light Touch’ National Framework for AI Policy
item.description: The next closest thing to no rules at all.

item.title: Windows 11 is finally getting a movable taskbar
item.description: It's taken almost five years, but Microsoft is finally bringing back the ability to move the Windows taskbar to the top or sides of your screen. The movable taskbar is part of some much bigger changes coming to Windows 11 this year in response to growing criticism of the operating system's performance, reliability, and user […]

item.title: Microsoft is ending the Windows Update nightmare — and letting you pause them indefinitely
item.description: In 2015, Microsoft decided that you shouldn't be in control of updating your PC anymore. At first, it seemed like a good idea to keep malware at bay - but soon, users discovered their computers were automatically shutting down and erasing work in the middle of the day. Then, Microsoft abused its power to install […]

item.title: Businesses are being 'locked in' to all-in-one platforms – and its costing them growth and adaptability
item.description: New research shows that companies are being held back by being locked into single ecosystems – but switching isn't easy.

item.title: This is Microsoft’s plan to fix Windows 11
item.description: Microsoft has faced a breakdown of trust in Windows 11 and a backlash over AI additions to its operating system in recent months. After promising to rebuild trust in Windows earlier this year, Microsoft's Windows chief, Pavan Davuluri, is now revealing the company's plan to fix Windows 11 - and there are a lot of […]

item.title: Three people have been charged with illegally exporting NVIDIA GPUs to China
item.description: The US Attorney's Office for the Southern District of New York has charged three people with illegally exporting NVIDIA GPUs to China in violation of the Export Control Reform Act. NVIDIA's chips have become a critical component in the rush to train and run increasingly complex artificial intelligence models, one the US has sought to manipulate with export controls and profit-sharing schemes with NVIDIA.The three people, Yih-Shyan "Wally" Liaw, Ruei-Tsang "Steven" Chang and Ting-Wei "Willy" Sun, two employees and one contractor working for US IT company Super Micro Computer, allegedly circumve...

item.title: Cet écran PC Acer (QD-OLED et 240 Hz) de 27″ va plaire aux gamers avec 150 € de moins
item.description: Les joueurs aguerris visent toujours des écrans PC bien réactifs et fluides. Ceux-là devraient donc être satisfaits de l'Acer Predator X27UW3bmiiprx, qui coche bien des cases, et qu’on trouve à 449 euros au lieu de 599 euros de base.
